title: Rational Root Theorem Bug Fix: Finding Irrational Roots

statement.markdown: Given a polynomial of degree `n`, your task is to find all its roots. However, this polynomial may have some irrational roots, and the Rational Root Theorem may not always apply.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a list of coefficients of the polynomial, from highest degree to lowest.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a list of roots, in the format `a ± sqrt(b)` if `a` and `b` are rational numbers.
statement.notes: You should avoid using any numerical methods or libraries.

hints.text: Use the Rational Root Theorem to find rational roots, if any.

editorial.title: Rational Root Theorem Bug Fix: Finding Irrational Roots
editorial.intuition: The Rational Root Theorem can be used to find rational roots, but it may not always apply. You should use the derivative of the polynomial to find the other roots.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to use a brute force method, such as trying all possible rational roots until you find some that satisfy the polynomial.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the derivative of the polynomial to find the other roots. You can do this by first finding the derivative of the polynomial, then solving for its roots. Once you have these roots, you can use them to find the roots of the original polynomial.
commonMistakes: Not using the Rational Root Theorem correctly
Not using the derivative of the polynomial correctly
